one_time_description: Specific Claims Settlement Trust revenue of $432,639 received during the year
economic_notes: Nekaneet First Nation incorporated NFNIHL on October 23, 2020 with two wholly owned subsidiaries (AB and SK corporations). Has 8.3% interest in Keseechiwan Holdings LP. TC Keystone Pipeline LP contributed $386,000 in revenue. New Horizon First Nations Administration Inc. is a government business enterprise accounted for on modified equity basis.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED - auditors unable to obtain sufficient audit evidence for Nekaneet First Nation Investment Holdings Limited and its two subsidiaries (NFNIHL reported revenue and expenses of $869,052 each). Province is Saskatchewan despite having an AB corporation subsidiary. New Horizon First Nations Administration Inc. is accounted for on modified equity basis as a GBE with a deficit equity of -$1,299,816 recorded as a reserve for losses. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $3,900,865 + FNIHB $1,229,557 + CMHC $149,475 + Trust funds $177,537 + FHQ Tribal Council $480,728 + Government of Saskatchewan tax rebate $16,263 + FSIN $5,274 + Yorkton Tribal Council $56,800 + Living Sky $18170 + Correctional Service Canada $160,598 = $7,195,267 approximately. Annual debt service includes LTD repayment $370,789 + CMHC repayment $91,086 = $461,875. Deferred revenue total is ISC $502,024 + FNIHB $170,335 + Other $430,251 = $1,102,610. Financial statements are for fiscal year ended March 31, 2021, not 2024.
